page: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g-5760s is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 96 AMD physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning compute resources are distributed among multiple tenants on the host system.

The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-capacity local storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation offers a high density of vCPUs and memory at a lower cost tier compared to dedicated instances, though it introduces the tradeoff of potential resource contention. This hardware profile is configured for general-purpose workloads, large-scale storage and database applications, and budget-conscious deployments requiring significant memory and local NVMe storage capacity without requiring dedicated CPU performance.
description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g-5760s is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 96 AMD physical cores and 96 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model. The system features 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 5760 GB NVMe SSD, while no GPU accelerators are present. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included. The shared CPU allocation presents a cost-efficient option for high-density resource requirements, though it introduces potential performance variability compared to dedicated instances. This configuration is suitable for large-scale databases, general-purpose workloads, and storage-heavy applications that can tolerate shared CPU resources.

tagline: A high-density shared-CPU instance offering ninety-six AMD cores, balanced memory, and extensive local NVMe SSD storage for cost-conscious workloads.
